summary: Multi-Metric Escalation Layer (Step 1) testing complete. All backend features working correctly. The 3-Gate model is implemented: Gate 1 (anomaly detected), Gate 2 (persistence >= 15min config), Gate 3 (score tier mapping). device_instability incident type created with correct metadata (source=multi_metric, tier, max_score, persistence info). All guardrails working: duplicate prevention, 30-min cooldown, simulation isolation. RBAC enforced (guardian blocked 403). L1_ONLY_INCIDENT_TYPES includes device_instability.

backend.update_persistence_minutes: PASS - Can update persistence_minutes via PUT
backend.update_escalation_tiers: PASS - Can update escalation_tiers via PUT
backend.update_instability_cooldown_minutes: PASS - Can update instability_cooldown_minutes via PUT
backend.duplicate_prevention: PASS - No duplicate incident created for same device
backend.cooldown_guardrail: PASS - No incident created within 30-min cooldown
backend.incident_metadata: PASS - device_instability incidents have correct metadata (source, tier, max_score, persistence_minutes_actual, etc.)

critical_code_review_comments: All Multi-Metric Escalation Layer Step 1 requirements met
3-Gate model correctly implemented: Gate 1 (anomaly detection), Gate 2 (persistence >= persistence_minutes), Gate 3 (score to tier mapping)
device_instability incident type created with correct metadata
Guardrails all working: duplicate prevention (no open incident), cooldown (30-min), simulation isolation (is_simulated=false)
device_instability added to L1_ONLY_INCIDENT_TYPES - no auto-escalation to L2/L3 notifications
combined_anomaly rule config updated with new fields: persistence_minutes, escalation_tiers, instability_cooldown_minutes
RBAC enforced - guardian blocked (403) from evaluate-instability endpoint
seed_data_creation: Created test multi_metric anomalies for Mob-01 to verify escalation flow (cleaned up after test)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-Metric Escalation Layer Step 1 fully tested. 22 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_instability_escalation.py cover: endpoint access, config fields, tier mapping, guardrails, simulation isolation. Two device_instability incidents exist in DB: one for WBAND-E2E-001 (resolved), one for Mob-01 (resolved, from this test). Cooldown is 30 minutes so new incidents for these devices won't be created until cooldown expires. The _evaluate_instability_escalation function runs as part of run_baseline_and_anomaly_cycle every 5 minutes.
